summary: Implementation plan for the 1985-NY-Yankees Azure Function: a nightly Timer-triggered Python 3.11 function that queries GPT-4o via TRAPI, validates and persists the roster to dedicated Blob Storage, with full IaC (Bicep), GitHub Actions CI/CD, Key Vault secret management, Application Insights alerting, and structured OpenTelemetry logging.
assumptions: The existing function_app.py and trapi_client.py are partial implementations; src/blob_writer.py and src/validator.py are referenced but not yet confirmed complete.
Infra Bicep modules exist partially (storage, function app, rbac, monitoring) per repository history but Key Vault module and full alert rules may be incomplete.
TRAPI endpoint https://trapi.research.microsoft.com/gcr/shared is reachable from the Azure Consumption Plan without VNet integration.
A specific GPT-4o deployment name/version for pinning will be confirmed by rciapala before APP-03 is completed.
Azure subscription and resource group for staging and production environments are pre-created or will be created as part of INFRA-01.
GitHub repository has OIDC/federated identity configured (or will be configured) for the GitHub Actions CD workflows.
Slot-based deployment (staging/production slots) is supported; Azure Functions Consumption Plan supports deployment slots.
prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt does not yet exist in source control.
host.json may need to be created or updated with functionTimeout=120s and maxConcurrentCalls=1.
Email notification recipient for alerts is rciapala (email address must be supplied for alert Action Group).
open_questions: What is the pinned GPT-4o deployment name/model version to use in TRAPI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME? (Required for deterministic output and prompt pinning.)
Has TRAPI Managed Identity auth (scope api://trapi/.default) been validated for this subscription/identity? Is there a separate onboarding step?
What Azure subscription and resource group names should be used for staging and production environments?
Does the Consumption Plan Function App support deployment slots in this subscription tier?
What is rciapala's email address for the Application Insights alert Action Group?
Is an existing Log Analytics Workspace available to attach App Insights to, or should a new one be provisioned?
Are there any egress/firewall restrictions on the Consumption Plan that would block calls to trapi.research.microsoft.com?
Should the Key Vault be zone-redundant (ZRS SKU) as specified, or is standard sufficient given the Consumption Plan SLA?

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Resolve design blockers, provision all Azure infrastructure, and establish CI/CD pipeline scaffolding so application development can proceed against real resources.
entry_criteria: spec.md reviewed and TRAPI endpoint/model version confirmed by owner
Azure subscription and resource group names confirmed for staging and production
GitHub repository OIDC federated identity credentials confirmed or planned
exit_criteria: All Azure resources provisioned in staging environment via Bicep
CD Infra workflow runs successfully end-to-end in staging
TRAPI Managed Identity auth validated manually from a test client
Key Vault created with TRAPI secret references wired into Function App settings
All design blockers (DES-01) resolved and documented in spec.md

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Implement all application modules, prompt template, host configuration, and CI/CD application workflows to produce a deployable, runnable function.
entry_criteria: TRAPI model version pinned and prompt template drafted
exit_criteria: src/validator.py and src/blob_writer.py implemented and importable
prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt committed to source control
host.json configured with functionTimeout=120s and maxConcurrentCalls=1
CI, CD App, and CD Promote GitHub Actions workflows present and syntactically valid
Function deploys to staging without errors via CD App workflow

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Achieve full test coverage, validate security controls, confirm observability stack, and verify end-to-end correctness in the staging environment.
entry_criteria: Core Build wave exit criteria met
Function deployed and running in staging
exit_criteria: Unit tests pass with coverage for validator, blob_writer, and trapi_client
Integration test confirms Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson appear in blob output
Security audit complete: no hardcoded secrets, all Key Vault references validated
All three alert rules active in Application Insights (failure, duration, data quality)
Structured log events and player_count_returned custom metric visible in App Insights
End-to-end staging run completes within 60 seconds with valid blob output

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Promote validated staging build to production, confirm monitoring is active, and complete all documentation for operational handoff.
entry_criteria: All unit and integration tests passing in CI
exit_criteria: Production deployment completed via CD Promote slot swap
First nightly production run succeeds and blob is written
All three alert rules confirmed active in production Application Insights
README.md complete with local dev setup, TRAPI auth, blob naming, and CI/CD federated identity instructions
All success criteria in spec.md checked off

tasks.title: Confirm TRAPI endpoint, GPT-4o model version, and Managed Identity auth scope
tasks.description: Validate that the TRAPI endpoint (https://trapi.research.microsoft.com/gcr/shared) is accessible, confirm the pinned GPT-4o deployment name/model version for TRAPI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME, and verify that Managed Identity bearer token auth with scope api://trapi/.default is supported. Document findings in spec.md under Resource Requirements (TRAPI endpoint row). Update the spec.md checkbox for TRAPI endpoint and add the confirmed deployment name. If Managed Identity is unsupported, document the fallback (Key Vault-stored credential) and ensure INFRA-02 provisions that secret.
outputs: Updated spec.md with confirmed TRAPI_ENDPOINT, TRAPI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME, and auth scope
Decision record on Key Vault fallback if Managed Identity auth is unsupported
acceptance_criteria: spec.md TRAPI endpoint checkbox is checked with confirmed URL
TRAPI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME (pinned GPT-4o model version) is documented in spec.md
Auth scope api://trapi/.default confirmed working with a test token acquisition
If Managed Identity auth is unsupported, a fallback secret strategy is documented and INFRA-02 updated accordingly
risks: TRAPI Managed Identity auth may require tenant-specific onboarding that blocks all TRAPI-dependent tasks
GPT-4o model version may be deprecated or unavailable, requiring a different deployment name

tasks.title: Provision Function App, dedicated Storage Account, and RBAC via Bicep
tasks.description: Ensure infra/main.bicep and its modules (storage.bicep, functionapp.bicep, rbac.bicep) provision: a Linux Consumption Plan Python 3.11 Function App with system-assigned Managed Identity; a dedicated Standard_LRS Cool-tier storage account with yankees-roster container (private, soft-delete 7 days); a separate host storage account; Storage Blob Data Contributor RBAC scoped to the yankees-roster container; and Queue Data Contributor for host storage. Validate that allowSharedKeyAccess:false is set and AzureWebJobsStorage uses identity-based connection.
acceptance_criteria: Bicep lints without errors (az bicep build)
Dedicated storage account provisioned with Standard_LRS, Cool access tier, allowSharedKeyAccess:false, soft-delete enabled 7 days
yankees-roster container exists with publicAccess:None
Function App provisions with system-assigned Managed Identity and Python 3.11 Linux Consumption Plan
Storage Blob Data Contributor role assigned to Function App identity scoped to yankees-roster container
AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName setting present (no connection string with key)
Deployment succeeds via 'az deployment group create' in staging
risks: Consumption Plan slot support may require specific SKU or region
allowSharedKeyAccess:false may break Azure Functions host if identity-based storage is misconfigured

tasks.title: Provision Azure Key Vault with TRAPI secret references and Key Vault Secrets User RBAC
tasks.description: Add infra/modules/keyvault.bicep to provision a zone-redundant Key Vault with Key Vault Secrets User RBAC for the Function App Managed Identity. Store TRAPI_ENDPOINT and TRAPI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME (and any fallback TRAPI credential if Managed Identity auth is unsupported) as secrets. Wire Key Vault references (@Microsoft.KeyVault(...)) into Function App App Settings in functionapp.bicep. Ensure RBAC authorization model (not access policies) is used.
outputs: infra/modules/functionapp.bicep (updated with KV references for TRAPI settings)
acceptance_criteria: Key Vault provisioned with enableRbacAuthorization:true and zone-redundant SKU
Key Vault Secrets User role assigned to Function App Managed Identity
TRAPI_ENDPOINT and TRAPI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME App Settings use @Microsoft.KeyVault(...) references
Function App can resolve KV references at runtime (verified via Azure Portal or az functionapp config appsettings list)
No plaintext secrets in Bicep files or App Settings
Bicep lints and deploys without errors
risks: Key Vault reference resolution requires Function App to have identity assigned before KV RBAC is applied — ordering must be correct in Bicep
Zone-redundant KV SKU may not be available in all regions

tasks.title: Provision Application Insights alert rules (failure, duration, data quality)
tasks.description: Extend infra/modules/alerts.bicep to add three Scheduled Query Rule alerts: (1) execution failure count > 0 in 1-hour window; (2) function execution duration > 90 seconds; (3) custom metric player_count_returned < 24 or > 40. Wire an email Action Group for rciapala. Ensure the existing exception alert is not duplicated. Connect all alerts to the App Insights workspace provisioned in monitoring.bicep.
outputs: infra/modules/alerts.bicep (updated with 3 alert rules + email Action Group)
acceptance_criteria: Three alert rules deployed: failure count, duration > 90s, player_count_returned out of range
Email Action Group targets rciapala email address
Alerts verified active in Azure Portal after deployment
No duplicate alerts from existing alerts.bicep rules
Bicep lints and deploys without errors
risks: player_count_returned custom metric alert requires the metric to be emitted at least once before the alert can be validated
rciapala email address must be supplied as a parameter to avoid hardcoding in Bicep

tasks.title: Implement CD Infra GitHub Actions workflow
tasks.description: Create .github/workflows/cd-infra.yml: manually triggered workflow with environment parameter (staging/prod). Uses OIDC federated identity (no stored secrets) to authenticate to Azure. Runs 'az deployment group create' for infra/main.bicep targeting the specified environment resource group. Runs a post-deploy validation step (checks all resources exist and role assignments are correct). README.md must document how to set up the federated identity credential for the workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Workflow triggers manually with environment input parameter
Azure authentication uses azure/login with OIDC (client-id, tenant-id, subscription-id from environment secrets — no client secrets)
Bicep deployment runs without errors for staging environment
Post-deploy validation step confirms Function App, Storage, KV, and App Insights exist
No secrets, API keys, or connection strings stored in workflow file or repository secrets
README.md includes federated identity setup instructions
risks: OIDC federated identity setup requires Azure AD app registration and trust configuration which may need admin access
Environment-specific resource group names must be parameterized correctly

tasks.title: Implement src/validator.py for roster schema and player count validation
tasks.description: Create src/validator.py implementing validate_roster_response() that validates the TRAPI response against the expected schema ({players: [{name, position, jersey_number}]}) and checks player count is between 24 and 28. Export ValidationErrorKind enum and ValidationResult dataclass consumed by both trapi_client.py and function_app.py. Handle missing fields, wrong types, and out-of-range counts as distinct error kinds.
acceptance_criteria: validate_roster_response() accepts a dict and returns a ValidationResult with is_valid, players, and optional error fields
ValidationErrorKind covers at minimum: SCHEMA_INVALID, PLAYER_COUNT_LOW, PLAYER_COUNT_HIGH
Valid payload with 24–28 players returns is_valid=True
Payload with wrong schema returns is_valid=False with SCHEMA_INVALID kind
Payload with < 24 players returns is_valid=False with PLAYER_COUNT_LOW kind
Payload with > 28 players returns is_valid=False with PLAYER_COUNT_HIGH kind
Module imports cleanly from both trapi_client.py and function_app.py
risks: TRAPI may return the roster nested inside a chat completion response wrapper rather than directly as {players:[...]}, requiring response unwrapping before validation

tasks.title: Implement src/blob_writer.py with conditional PUT and failed/ prefix support
tasks.description: Create src/blob_writer.py implementing BlobWriter class with write() and write_failed() methods. write() performs a conditional PUT using If-None-Match: * to prevent double-writes, returns blob URI on success or None on conflict. write_failed() writes to yankees-roster/failed/{run_date_utc}.json. Both methods authenticate via DefaultAzureCredential (Managed Identity). Storage account name and container name are read from environment variables (ROSTER_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME, ROSTER_CONTAINER_NAME).
acceptance_criteria: BlobWriter.write() uses BlobClient.upload_blob with overwrite=False (enforces If-None-Match: *) and returns blob URI string on success
BlobWriter.write() returns None (no exception) when blob already exists (ResourceExistsError caught)
BlobWriter.write_failed() writes to yankees-roster/failed/{run_date_utc}.json
Both methods use DefaultAzureCredential (no connection strings or account keys)
Storage account URL constructed from ROSTER_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME env var
Container name read from ROSTER_CONTAINER_NAME env var with default 'yankees-roster'
log event blob_write_succeeded emitted with blob URI
risks: Conditional PUT behavior with azure-storage-blob SDK must be verified — ResourceExistsError is raised when overwrite=False and blob exists
Managed Identity may not have role assignment ready at first deploy; startup error handling should be clear

tasks.title: Create prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt and configure host.json
tasks.description: Create prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt with a prompt instructing GPT-4o to return the 1985 New York Yankees active roster in the specified JSON schema ({players:[{name, position, jersey_number}]}). The prompt must be deterministic and request exactly the active 24–28 roster members. Create or update host.json to set functionTimeout to 120 seconds, maxConcurrentCalls to 1, and WEBSITE_TIME_ZONE / UTC timezone. Ensure local.settings.json.example includes all required env vars: TRAPI_ENDPOINT, TRAPI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME, TRAPI_AUTH_SCOPE, TRAPI_API_VERSION, ROSTER_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME, ROSTER_CONTAINER_NAME.
outputs: local.settings.json.example (updated with all required env vars)
acceptance_criteria: prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt exists at path matching PROMPT_PATH in trapi_client.py
Prompt instructs model to return JSON matching {players:[{name:string, position:string, jersey_number:integer}]}
Prompt requests active 24–28 roster members of the 1985 New York Yankees
host.json sets functionTimeout: '00:02:00' (120 seconds)
host.json sets extensions.queues.maxConcurrentCalls (or equivalent) to 1
local.settings.json.example includes TRAPI_ENDPOINT, TRAPI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME, TRAPI_AUTH_SCOPE, TRAPI_API_VERSION, ROSTER_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME, ROSTER_CONTAINER_NAME with placeholder values
risks: GPT-4o may not return consistent JSON without explicit JSON-mode or response_format instruction in the prompt/payload — consider adding response_format: {type: json_object} to the TRAPI request payload

tasks.title: Complete function_app.py: structured log events and OpenTelemetry custom metric
tasks.description: Review and complete function_app.py to ensure all five required structured log events are emitted: function_started, trapi_request_sent (model version, prompt hash), trapi_response_received (token count, latency ms, player count), blob_write_succeeded (blob URI), function_completed. Confirm player_count_returned OTel counter is incremented correctly. Verify imports from src.blob_writer and src.validator resolve after APP-01 and APP-02 are complete. Confirm use_monitor=True and schedule='0 0 2 * * *' are correct.
acceptance_criteria: All five structured log events emitted with correct extra fields per spec.md
player_count_returned OTel counter incremented with run_date_utc attribute on each successful run
use_monitor=True confirmed in @app.timer_trigger decorator
schedule='0 0 2 * * *' confirmed
Function imports resolve cleanly (python -c 'import function_app' succeeds with mocked env vars)
write_conflict=True logged when blob already exists (None return from BlobWriter.write)
risks: OpenTelemetry SDK may require azure-monitor-opentelemetry exporter configuration in function_app.py startup; verify APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING is set

tasks.title: Implement CI GitHub Actions workflow (unit tests on push to main)
tasks.description: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml: triggered on push to main, disabled by default (workflow_dispatch or manual enable). Installs Python 3.11, installs requirements.txt, runs pytest with coverage. Uses no Azure credentials (pure unit test run). Workflow must pass before merge is allowed.
acceptance_criteria: Workflow triggers on push to main
Workflow is disabled by default (uses workflow_dispatch trigger or has 'if: false' guard until enabled)
Python 3.11 environment set up with pip install -r requirements.txt
pytest runs and exits 0 when all tests pass
Coverage report generated and visible in workflow output
No Azure credentials or secrets in workflow file
risks: Disabled-by-default mechanism must be clearly documented so it can be enabled after staging validation

tasks.title: Implement CD App GitHub Actions workflow (manual deploy + smoke test)
tasks.description: Create .github/workflows/cd-app.yml: manually triggered with environment parameter (staging/prod). Uses OIDC federated identity to authenticate to Azure. Deploys function app code via 'az functionapp deployment source config-zip' or Azure Functions Action. Runs a post-deploy smoke test (triggers function manually and checks for blob creation or log event). No secrets or API keys stored in workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Workflow triggers manually with environment input (staging or prod)
Azure authentication uses OIDC (no client secrets in workflow or repo secrets)
Function app code deployed successfully to the target environment
Smoke test step runs after deploy and exits 0 on success
Smoke test verifies function can be triggered and blob appears in staging storage
Workflow exits non-zero on smoke test failure
risks: Smoke test requires the function to complete a full TRAPI call; if TRAPI is unavailable in CI, a mock or health-only smoke test may be needed

tasks.title: Implement CD Promote GitHub Actions workflow (staging to prod slot swap)
tasks.description: Create .github/workflows/cd-promote.yml: manually triggered with source (default: staging) and target (default: prod) environment parameters. Uses OIDC federated identity. Performs Azure Functions deployment slot swap from source to target. Includes pre-swap validation (confirm source slot is healthy) and post-swap smoke test on production.
acceptance_criteria: Workflow triggers manually with source and target environment inputs (defaults: staging, prod)
Pre-swap step validates source slot is in a healthy/running state
Slot swap performed via az functionapp deployment slot swap
Post-swap smoke test verifies production slot is responding
Azure authentication uses OIDC only
Workflow exits non-zero if pre-swap validation or post-swap smoke test fails
risks: Azure Functions Consumption Plan may have limited slot support; confirm slot availability before implementing swap logic

tasks.title: Write unit tests for validator, blob_writer, and trapi_client
tasks.description: Create tests/ directory with unit tests covering: src/validator.py (valid payload, schema errors, count low, count high, known players present); src/blob_writer.py (successful write returns URI, conflict returns None, failed write path); trapi_client.py (retry logic, exponential backoff, fatal status codes, retry exhausted error, prompt loading and hashing). Use pytest with mocks for HTTP calls and Azure SDK. Achieve >80% coverage on all three modules.
acceptance_criteria: pytest runs all tests and passes with exit code 0
>80% line coverage on src/validator.py, src/blob_writer.py, trapi_client.py
Test asserts Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson are accepted as valid players
Test asserts payload with 23 players fails with PLAYER_COUNT_LOW
Test asserts payload with 29 players fails with PLAYER_COUNT_HIGH
Blob write conflict (ResourceExistsError) returns None without exception
TRAPI 429 triggers retry with backoff; 401 raises immediately without retry
All tests runnable without Azure credentials (fully mocked)
risks: azure-storage-blob mock setup can be complex; use unittest.mock.patch on BlobClient methods

tasks.title: Write integration test verifying known players appear in blob output
tasks.description: Create tests/integration/test_roster_integration.py: an integration test that triggers the function against the staging environment (or a local Azure Functions host with real TRAPI credentials) and reads the output blob from the staging storage account. Asserts that the blob exists, is valid JSON, and contains Mattingly, Winfield, and Henderson. Mark as integration test (requires TRAPI access and Azure credentials) and skip in CI unit test run unless RUN_INTEGRATION_TESTS=true.
acceptance_criteria: Integration test connects to staging storage account using DefaultAzureCredential
Test triggers function and waits for blob to appear (polling with timeout)
Test asserts blob at yankees-roster/{today_utc}.json exists and is valid JSON
Test asserts players list contains entries with name matching Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson
Test is skipped when RUN_INTEGRATION_TESTS env var is not set
Test passes end-to-end in staging environment
risks: Integration test depends on TRAPI availability; flaky if TRAPI is degraded
Timer Trigger cannot be directly invoked via HTTP without an HTTP trigger wrapper; may need to use 'az functionapp function invoke' or add a temporary HTTP trigger for testing

tasks.title: Security audit: verify no hardcoded secrets, KV references, and private container
tasks.description: Perform a security audit across all source files, Bicep templates, GitHub Actions workflows, and configuration files. Verify: no hardcoded secrets or connection strings anywhere; all TRAPI and storage credentials flow through Managed Identity or Key Vault references; yankees-roster container has publicAccess:None; allowSharedKeyAccess:false on storage account; OIDC-only auth in all GitHub Actions workflows. Run a secret scanning tool (e.g., git-secrets or truffleHog) as part of the audit.
outputs: Security audit findings documented (in PR or issue comments)
acceptance_criteria: No hardcoded secrets, API keys, or connection strings found in any committed file
local.settings.json is in .gitignore (only .example is committed)
All GitHub Actions workflows use OIDC federated identity (no stored client secrets)
Bicep confirms allowSharedKeyAccess:false and publicAccess:None on container
Key Vault references (@Microsoft.KeyVault(...)) used for all sensitive App Settings
Secret scanning tool returns 0 findings on HEAD commit
risks: Developer may have accidentally committed local.settings.json with real credentials; git history scan required

tasks.title: Validate structured logging and OpenTelemetry custom metric in Application Insights
tasks.description: Deploy to staging and validate that all five structured log events (function_started, trapi_request_sent, trapi_response_received, blob_write_succeeded, function_completed) appear in Application Insights with correct custom dimensions. Validate that the player_count_returned custom metric is visible in App Insights Metrics Explorer. Confirm Application Insights sampling is enabled. Verify log retention is set to 30 days on the Log Analytics Workspace.
outputs: OPS validation report (PR comment or issue comment confirming each log event and metric is visible)
acceptance_criteria: All five log events visible in App Insights with correct custom dimension fields
player_count_returned metric visible in App Insights Metrics Explorer after a test run
Log Analytics Workspace retention confirmed at 30 days
Application Insights sampling enabled (visible in App Insights configuration)
All three alert rules show as 'Enabled' in Azure Monitor
Test alert fire verified for at least one rule (e.g., trigger a deliberate failure)
risks: OpenTelemetry metric export to App Insights requires APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING to be set and azure-monitor-opentelemetry properly configured; verify exporter setup in function_app.py

tasks.title: End-to-end staging validation (nightly run verified, smoke test passing)
tasks.description: Trigger the function manually in staging (via az functionapp function invoke or CD App workflow), verify the full execution path: TRAPI call succeeds, response validated, blob written to yankees-roster/{date}.json, all log events appear in App Insights, player_count_returned metric emitted. Confirm a second trigger on the same day results in a write_conflict=True log (idempotency). Verify missed execution alert is not firing spuriously.
inputs: staging environment fully provisioned and deployed
outputs: Staging validation sign-off (PR comment or issue comment)
acceptance_criteria: Function completes within 60 seconds in staging
Blob yankees-roster/{today_utc}.json exists with valid JSON and 24–28 players
Known players (Mattingly, Winfield, Henderson) present in blob
Second trigger on same date returns write_conflict=True in logs and no duplicate blob
All five log events visible in App Insights for the run
player_count_returned metric value between 24 and 28
No alerts firing spuriously after successful run
risks: TRAPI may be unavailable or rate-limited during staging test; have a fallback plan (mock TRAPI response) for validation

tasks.title: Complete README.md with local dev setup, TRAPI auth, blob naming, and CI/CD federated identity
tasks.description: Update README.md to include: (1) local development setup (Python 3.11, venv, pip install, local.settings.json configuration); (2) TRAPI authentication instructions (how to obtain a bearer token locally, Managed Identity requirements); (3) blob naming convention (yankees-roster/{run_date_utc}.json, failed/ prefix); (4) CI/CD setup with Azure federated identity (step-by-step instructions for creating the Azure AD app registration, federated credential, and GitHub secrets CLIENT_ID, TENANT_ID, SUBSCRIPTION_ID); (5) how to run unit tests and integration tests.
acceptance_criteria: README.md has a Local Development section with step-by-step setup instructions
README.md documents all required environment variables with descriptions
README.md explains TRAPI auth scope and how to obtain a local bearer token for testing
README.md explains blob naming convention including failed/ prefix
README.md has CI/CD section with federated identity setup instructions (az ad app create, federated credential, GitHub secrets)
README.md documents how to run unit tests (pytest) and integration tests (RUN_INTEGRATION_TESTS=true)

tasks.title: Production deployment via CD Promote slot swap and production validation
tasks.description: Run CD Promote workflow to swap staging slot to production. Verify production Function App is running, all App Settings resolve correctly (KV references), and Application Insights is connected. Wait for first nightly production run (2:00 AM UTC) or trigger manually. Confirm blob written to production storage, all alerts active, and monitoring dashboards showing healthy metrics. Mark all spec.md success criteria as complete.
acceptance_criteria: CD Promote workflow completes successfully with exit code 0
Production Function App status is 'Running'
All Key Vault references resolve in production App Settings
Production Application Insights shows function execution within 60 seconds
Blob written to production yankees-roster container with correct naming
All three alert rules active in production Application Insights
No errors in production Application Insights for first 24 hours post-deploy
risks: First production nightly run at 2:00 AM UTC cannot be validated until that time; consider manual trigger for initial validation
Slot swap cold start on Consumption Plan may cause first execution to be slow; verify within timeout
